notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants

evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime: first spike lands around minute 11 (Crescelia), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +16.5% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Unholy Pursuit
augments.note: soul economy and conditional cooldown reset

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 8,691 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 557 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.5× the 10s rotation damage: try Crescelia over Fire Blossom (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oathkeeper: their 3s all-in loses 28% of its bite; you give up 17% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.
